subject: UX & Research
title: The Citibank $900 Million Mistake
description: One-minute skill: The Citibank $900 Million Mistake.

hook.text: In 2020, Citibank accidentally wired $900 million to lenders. A confusing UI form was the cause.

buildup.text: An operator meant to send a small interest payment. The software's form had three checkboxes in a confusing layout.

discovery.text: The principal payment checkbox was checked by default. The operator didn't notice, and $900 million went out.

twist.text: A court ruled the recipients could keep the money. Citibank couldn't recover most of the funds.

climax.text: Dangerous defaults and confusing layouts cost real money. High-stakes forms need extra confirmation steps.

punchline.text: Bad form design costs fortunes. High-stakes actions need safeguards.

quiz.question: What caused Citibank's $900 million accident?
options: Confusing form layout with a dangerous default checkbox
A hacker exploiting a security vulnerability
A server crash during a transaction
